executiveSummary.text: Experian plc (EXPGF) is now back in sync with the canonical DYOR HQ sheet after the metadata repair pass. The current stance is REDUCE with conviction 48/100, using $35.04 as the latest stored price reference where available.

businessModel.text: Experian plc sits in data, analytics and consumer credit infrastructure, where recurring revenues, data quality and regulatory discipline underpin the franchise.

recentCatalysts.text: Credit demand, analytics adoption, pricing and product expansion remain the main drivers.

thesisEvaluation.text: Base case: the core operating backdrop remains good enough to support the present reduce stance, but not strong enough to justify a looser risk view without better evidence. Bull case: execution and demand improve faster than expected. Bear case: operating or funding pressure worsens and compresses valuation further.

keyRisks.text: Regulatory scrutiny can raise compliance costs. Credit-cycle weakness can affect transaction volumes. Competition in analytics can pressure pricing. Cybersecurity and data-governance risks remain material. Execution on new products can lag expectations.

risks.risk: Regulatory scrutiny can raise compliance costs.
Credit-cycle weakness can affect transaction volumes.
Competition in analytics can pressure pricing.
Cybersecurity and data-governance risks remain material.
Execution on new products can lag expectations.

whoShouldOwn.text: Own: Investors who favour data-rich recurring-revenue businesses. Avoid: Investors uncomfortable with credit-cycle and data-regulation risk.

recommendation.text: REDUCE - Conviction: 48/100. This rebuilt page is intentionally concise, but it is now internally coherent and aligned to the corrected ticker and company metadata.

entryExit.text: Keep position sizing consistent with a reduce stance until fuller company-specific analysis is refreshed.
